description:
You are the Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) at VertexCloud Technologies, a SaaS platform provider serving 3,200 enterprise clients. Three days ago, your organization suffered a ransomware attack attributed to the LockBit 3.0 gang. Attackers gained initial access through an unpatched VPN appliance, pivoted laterally to the backup server, encrypted 4.2TB of production and backup data, and demanded $2.1M in ransom. Recovery operations ran for 72 hours using offline cold backups.

Using the credentials at /home/ga/eramba/credentials.txt, document the incident and its remediation in Eramba. Create a security incident record with 'Ransomware' in the title, detailing the initial access method, affected data, business impact, and the attacker group. Based on the root cause analysis, create at least three post-incident risks with treatment strategies covering unpatched network perimeter devices, lack of offline/immutable backup storage, and absence of network microsegmentation. Identify and create at least four new internal controls (Security Services) to address gaps discovered during the incident, such as immutable backup storage, zero-trust architecture, or patch management SLAs. Document at least two policy exceptions for emergency security control bypasses made during the 72-hour recovery period. Finally, create a project with 'Recovery' or 'Ransomware' in the title to track all post-incident remediation work.
